name: plasticc

env_axis.name: representativeness
env_axis.shift_mechanism: train set deliberately non-representative (nearby + DDF + spec-confirmed); test simulates fair LSST 10-yr survey; class-99 is novel-class holdout

facts: 7,848 training + 3,492,890 test light curves across 14 classes + class-99 unknown; training set non-representative by design (spec-confirmed DDF).
hypotheses: Class-99 (novel-class) detection is categorically distinct from covariate shift, rather than something strong covariate methods solve incidentally.
The deliberate train/test non-representativeness produces a measurable shift that methods can characterize, rather than methods merely learning class proxies.
intuitions: A 2024-25 photometric-classification literature likely exists following LSST first light.
ZTF live-test comparisons probably exist that serve as a post-PLAsTiCC reality check.
unknowns: Class-99 lumps all unknowns into one bucket, so uncertainty stratification by type versus by instance is not represented in the schema.
The training-label modality (spec-confirmation) categorically differs from the inference modality (photometric); whether this is part of the OOD shift or simply label noise cannot be determined.
epistemic_state.boundary: Mixed origin (partly simulated); photometric-only; class-99 is monolithic, so the dataset is silent on within-novel-class structure.

materialization_status: training set + test_metadata only (180 MB); test_lightcurves 01-11 (each ~700 MB, total ~8 GB) deferred
